name: 6q16_microdeletion_SIM1_PWS_like
description: 6q16 microdeletion syndrome — recurrent ~3-7 Mb interstitial deletion at 6q16.1-q16.3 causing a Prader-Willi-like phenotype (obesity, hyperphagia, hypotonia, ID, characteristic facies). Critical gene SIM1 (haploinsufficiency = obesity, autonomic dysfunction). Some breakpoints flanked by SD blocks at 6q16, others non-recurrent.

architecture.internal_structure: Flanking SD blocks of ~20-100 kb at >95% identity; unique core contains SIM1 (primary dosage gene; obesity), POU3F2, MCHR2, GRIK2, PREP, GJA1, ASCC3. SIM1 haploinsufficiency alone reproduces the obesity phenotype.
